concerns.text: The plan cites 'ResearchClawBench' (arXiv:2606.07591) but explicitly states 'NO verified source found' and assumes local availability. The Constitution (Principle I) requires external datasets to be fetched from canonical sources. Relying on a local copy without a verified URL/loader prevents reproducibility on a fresh runner. The plan must identify the canonical repository (e.g., Hugging Face, Zenodo, or GitHub) and provide the specific access method/loader to satisfy the 'Verified Accuracy' and 'Reproducibility' principles.

The plan assumes 7 distinct autonomous agents from the original study can be executed on CPU-only GitHub Actions runners. If these agents rely on large language models (LLMs) requiring GPU acceleration or significant VRAM (common in 'autonomous agent' benchmarks), the 'CPU-only' constraint makes the experimental execution infeasible. The plan must verify the compute requirements of the specific 7 agents or define a CPU-compatible subset/quantization strategy to ensure the 140 runs can actually complete within the 24-hour budget.
